---
title: "bapq initial stuff"
output: html_document
---

---
title: "BAPQ"
output: html_document
date: "2026-02-17"
---

```{r script settings and resources, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE) #can i delete this or will it kill my code?
library(tidyverse)
library(dplyr)
library(plyr)
library(patchwork)

data_dir <- "G:/My Drive/Mouse data spring 2026/data"
file_list <- list.files(
  path = data_dir,
  pattern = "\\.csv$",
  full.names = TRUE
  )
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r data import and cleaning}
#i fink that this stuff is all necessary but i commented out the write.csv code because i like my csv files as is >:(
master_df <- lapply(file_list, read_csv, show_col_types = FALSE) %>%
  bind_rows()
master_df <- master_df %>%
  select(!c(internal_node_id, participantCode, trial_type, subject, success)) %>%
  filter(!is.na(test_part)) #separating by test part
bandit_master <- read.csv("G:/My Drive/Mouse data spring 2026/master_df.csv") %>%
  mutate(key_press = factor(key_press, levels = c("37", "38", "39"))) #recoding key press
bandit_master <- bandit_master %>%
  mutate(
    #chance = rowMeans(across(c(leftProb, midProb, rightProb))), 
    outcome = as.integer(correct), 
    stay = as.integer(key_press == lag(key_press)), 
    chosen_prob = case_when(
    key_press == "37" ~ leftProb,
    key_press == "38" ~ midProb,
    key_press == "39" ~ rightProb,
  ),
  best_choice = case_when(
    chosen_prob == pmax(leftProb, midProb, rightProb) ~ 1,
    chosen_prob != pmax(leftProb, midProb, rightProb) ~ 0
  ))#doing some calculations of probabilities 
master_df <- master_df %>% mutate_at('rt', as.numeric) #fixing the RT variable from the master_df (individual scores, not means) file
bandit_master <- bandit_master %>%
  group_by(qualtrics_rid, test_part)%>%
  summarise(
    pReward = mean(outcome),
    pChance = mean(chance),
    reward_minus_chance = pReward - pChance,
    pBest = mean(best_choice)
  )

```

## Checking normality

You can also embed plots, for example:

```{r choice bias, echo=FALSE}
master_df %>%
  filter(test_part == "restless") %>%
  mutate(qualtrics_rid = factor(qualtrics_rid)) %>%
  group_by(qualtrics_rid, key_press) %>%
  summarise(n = n()) %>%
  View()

choice_wide <- master_df %>%
  count(qualtrics_rid, test_part, key_press) %>%
  group_by(qualtrics_rid, test_part) %>%
  mutate(prop = n / sum(n)) %>%
  select(-n) %>%
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = key_press,
    values_from = prop,
    values_fill = 0,
    names_prefix = "p_"
  ) %>%
  rename(
    p_left   = p_37,
    p_center = p_38,
    p_right  = p_39
  )

#choice bias plot <- save this later
# restless_choice_bias <- choice_wide %>%
#   filter(test_part == "restless") %>%
#   group_by(qualtrics_rid) %>%
#   mutate(max_bias = pmax(p_left, p_center, p_right)) %>%
#   ggplot(aes(max_bias)) + geom_histogram() + ggtitle("restless_choice_bias")
# 
# spatial_choice_bias <- choice_wide %>%
#   filter(test_part == "spatial") %>%
#   group_by(qualtrics_rid) %>%
#   mutate(max_bias = pmax(p_left, p_center, p_right)) %>%
#   ggplot(aes(max_bias)) + geom_histogram() + ggtitle("spatial_choice_bias")
# 
# vis1_choice_bias <- choice_wide %>%
#   filter(test_part == "vis1") %>%
#   group_by(qualtrics_rid) %>%
#   mutate(max_bias = pmax(p_left, p_center, p_right)) %>%
#   ggplot(aes(max_bias)) + geom_histogram() + ggtitle("vis1_choice_bias")
# 
# vis2_choice_bias <- choice_wide %>%
#   filter(test_part == "vis2") %>%
#   group_by(qualtrics_rid) %>%
#   mutate(max_bias = pmax(p_left, p_center, p_right)) %>%
#   ggplot(aes(max_bias)) + geom_histogram() + ggtitle("vis2_choice_bias")
# 
# restless_choice_bias + spatial_choice_bias + vis1_choice_bias + vis2_choice_bias
####^^^ slower way of doing this
choice_wide %>%
  #filter(test_part == "vis1") %>%
  group_by(qualtrics_rid) %>%
  mutate(max_bias = pmax(p_left, p_center, p_right)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(max_bias)) + geom_histogram() + ggtitle("choice_bias") + facet_wrap(~ test_part)
```
choice bias overall demonstrates a relatively "normal" distribution of side biases, with spatial having the greatest tail (expected), so it doesn't seem like this is going to be a significant problem. it might be a significant problem though, we'll have to cross reference with reaction times though

```{r pReward}
#preward by test_part
mu <- ddply(bandit_master, "test_part", summarise, grp.mean=mean(pReward))

ggplot(bandit_master, aes(x=pReward)) + geom_histogram(binwidth = ) + facet_grid(test_part ~.) + geom_vline(data=mu, aes(xintercept=grp.mean, color="red"),linetype="dashed") +  ggtitle("pReward Distribution") 

#checking normality

shapiro.test(bandit_master$pReward) #pReward is not normally distributed. p = 0.03835, w = 0.9933

results <- group_by(bandit_master, test_part)
head(results)
shapiro.test(results$pReward)

bandit_master <- bandit_master %>%
  mutate(test_part = factor(test_part))

bandit_master %>%
  filter(test_part == "restless") %>%
  summarise(W =  shapiro.test(pReward)$statistic,
            p = shapiro.test(pReward)$p.value) #normal! w = .9881, p = 0.4062

bandit_master %>%
  filter(test_part == "spatial") %>%
  summarise(W =  shapiro.test(pReward)$statistic,
            p = shapiro.test(pReward)$p.value) #normal! w = .9876, p = .3735

bandit_master %>%
  filter(test_part == "vis1") %>%
  summarise(W =  shapiro.test(pReward)$statistic,
            p = shapiro.test(pReward)$p.value) #not normal w = .9742 p = 0.0246

bandit_master %>%
  filter(test_part == "vis2") %>%
  summarise(W =  shapiro.test(pReward)$statistic,
            p = shapiro.test(pReward)$p.value) #normal! w = 0.9846, p = 0.2093
```
not normally distributed overall. What about by test_part -> outlier is vis1 who is not normal, everyone else has normal pReward

```{r pChance}
mu <- ddply(bandit_master, "test_part", summarise, grp.mean=mean(pChance))

ggplot(bandit_master, aes(x=pChance)) + geom_histogram() + facet_grid(test_part ~.) + geom_vline(data=mu, aes(xintercept=grp.mean, color="red"),linetype="dashed") +  ggtitle("pChance Distribution")

#um i guess i'll do a shapiro-wilk on restless since the rest of them are apparently unimodal with one value.
bandit_master %>%
  filter(test_part == "restless") %>%
  summarise(W =  shapiro.test(pChance)$statistic,
            p = shapiro.test(pChance)$p.value) #normally distributed. w=0.9905 p=0.6078

```

this is correct for chance

```{r reward_minus_chance}
mu <- ddply(bandit_master, "test_part", summarise, grp.mean=mean(reward_minus_chance))

ggplot(bandit_master, aes(x=reward_minus_chance)) + geom_histogram() + facet_grid(test_part ~.) + geom_vline(data=mu, aes(xintercept=grp.mean, color="red"),linetype="dashed") +  ggtitle("reward_minus_chance Distribution")

bandit_master %>%
  filter(test_part == "restless") %>%
  summarise(W =  shapiro.test(reward_minus_chance)$statistic,
            p = shapiro.test(reward_minus_chance)$p.value) #normal w=0.9817 p=0.1140

bandit_master %>%
  filter(test_part == "spatial") %>%
  summarise(W =  shapiro.test(reward_minus_chance)$statistic,
            p = shapiro.test(reward_minus_chance)$p.value) #normal w=0.9876 p=0.3735

bandit_master %>%
  filter(test_part == "vis1") %>%
  summarise(W =  shapiro.test(reward_minus_chance)$statistic,
            p = shapiro.test(reward_minus_chance)$p.value) #not normal w=0.9742 p=0.0246

bandit_master %>%
  filter(test_part == "vis2") %>%
  summarise(W =  shapiro.test(reward_minus_chance)$statistic,
            p = shapiro.test(reward_minus_chance)$p.value) #normal w=0.9846 p=0.2093
```
normality here tracks since the pReward values were not normal (a-normal?) for vis1

```{r pBest}
mu <- ddply(bandit_master, "test_part", summarise, grp.mean=mean(pBest))

ggplot(bandit_master, aes(x=pBest)) + geom_histogram()  + geom_vline(data=mu, aes(xintercept=grp.mean, color="red"),linetype="dashed") +  ggtitle("pBest Distribution")


bandit_master %>%
  filter(test_part == "vis2") %>%
  group_by(qualtrics_rid) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x=pBest)) + geom_histogram()
  
  
bandit_master %>%
  filter(test_part == "restless") %>%
  summarise(W =  shapiro.test(pBest)$statistic,
            p = shapiro.test(pBest)$p.value) #normal w=0.9891 p=0.4828
bandit_master %>%
  filter(test_part == "spatial") %>%
  summarise(W =  shapiro.test(pBest)$statistic,
            p = shapiro.test(pBest)$p.value) #NOT normal like at all like so incredibly not normal w=0.9440 p=0.0001
bandit_master %>%
  filter(test_part == "vis1") %>%
  summarise(W =  shapiro.test(pBest)$statistic,
            p = shapiro.test(pBest)$p.value) #wow it gets worse w=0.8651 p=7.2188 x10^-9
bandit_master %>%
  filter(test_part == "vis2") %>%
  summarise(W =  shapiro.test(pBest)$statistic,
            p = shapiro.test(pBest)$p.value) #not normal w=0.8863 p=6.2295x10^-8
```

```{r RT}
mu <- ddply(master_df, "test_part", summarise, grp.mean=mean(rt)) 

ggplot(master_df, aes(x=rt)) + geom_histogram() + facet_grid(test_part ~.) + geom_vline(data=mu, aes(xintercept=grp.mean, color="red"),linetype="dashed") +  ggtitle("rt Distribution")

master_df %>%
  filter(rt <= 5000) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x=rt)) + geom_histogram() + facet_grid(test_part ~.) + geom_vline(data=mu, aes(xintercept=grp.mean, color="red"),linetype="dashed") +  ggtitle("rt Distribution")

#per participant

bandit_master %>%
  mutate(qualtrics_rid = factor(qualtrics_rid)) %>%
  dplyr::group_by(test_part) %>%
  summarise(meanRT = mean(rt)) %>%
  View()

bandit_master %>%
  filter(test_part == "restless") %>%
  summarise(W =  shapiro.test(pBest)$statistic,
            p = shapiro.test(pBest)$p.value) #normal w=

```
YAY i love following tutorials online i will be linking this one here bc omg thank you internet https://stat212-learnr.stat.illinois.edu/L5.1_dplyr_introduction/#section-plotting-within-a-pipe i hate u university of illinois but ur stat department is locked in
